# Red Team: Paper Claims Example

This document was generated using Red Team methodology with H0 = "This is WRONG".

## Target Statement

> "Our proposed LSTM model achieves 95% accuracy on sales forecasting, outperforming all baseline methods by 20%."


## Attack Matrix


### Round 1: 5W1H Attack

| Q | Attack | Evidence | Result |
|---|--------|----------|--------|
| What | Is 95% accuracy correct? | Verified with Safe Math[^1] | PASS |
| Why | Why 20% improvement? | Baseline metrics unclear[^2] | CONCERNS |
| Where | Which dataset? | Dataset not fully described[^3] | CONCERNS |
| When | Time period valid? | Train/test split verified[^4] | PASS |
| Who | Author credentials? | Published in peer-reviewed[^5] | PASS |
| How | LSTM implementation? | Standard PyTorch code[^6] | PASS |


### Round 2: 3-Level Attack

| Level | Method | Attack | Evidence | Result |
|-------|--------|--------|----------|--------|
| L1 | Counter-example | Find case where LSTM fails | Sparsity 99.2% causes issues[^7] | FAIL |
| L2 | Contradiction | If 95% accurate, why high variance? | Variance 8.3% contradicts claim[^8] | CONCERNS |
| L3 | Null Hypothesis | H0: No improvement vs baseline | p-value = 0.032 < 0.05, reject H0[^9] | PASS |

## Concerns (if any)

1. **Metric Choice**: Accuracy is inappropriate for sparse data. Should use MASE or sMAPE[^14]

2. **Sparsity Handling**: 99.2% sparsity not adequately addressed by LSTM architecture[^7]

3. **Baseline Comparison**: Baseline methods (Naive, SNaive) may be too weak for fair comparison[^29]

4. **Single Dataset**: Generalization not tested on multiple datasets[^16]


## Recommendations

1. Re-evaluate using MASE metric instead of accuracy

2. Compare against Croston/SBA/TSB methods designed for sparse data

3. Test on additional sparse datasets to verify generalization

4. Provide detailed sparsity analysis and how LSTM handles it


## Verdict

**CONCERNS**

Rationale: While statistical significance achieved (p < 0.05), the choice of accuracy metric for 99.2% sparse data is problematic. LSTM may not be optimal for intermittent demand. Need additional validation with domain-appropriate metrics and baselines.
